risk_description: No formal policy exists governing when and how AI systems may be deployed across the organization
current_controls: Ad hoc review by individual teams
gaps_and_recommendations: Develop and ratify an enterprise AI acceptable use policy; define approval thresholds by risk tier

risk_description: Accountability for AI system outcomes is not formally assigned; no designated AI risk owner per system
current_controls: General product ownership via engineering leads
gaps_and_recommendations: Require a named AI System Owner for each deployed model; document in system inventory

risk_description: Staff involved in AI development and procurement lack formal training on algorithmic fairness and ethical AI
current_controls: Annual security awareness training (not AI-specific)
gaps_and_recommendations: Add AI ethics module to onboarding and annual training curriculum; track completion rates

risk_description: Decisions about AI deployment are siloed within product teams without legal privacy or compliance input
current_controls: Informal legal review on high-profile launches only
gaps_and_recommendations: Establish an AI Review Board with rotating representation from Legal Privacy Engineering and Compliance

risk_description: Vendor contracts do not require disclosure of AI model changes or performance degradation over time
current_controls: Standard vendor security questionnaire (not AI-specific)
gaps_and_recommendations: Develop AI-specific vendor addendum covering model versioning transparency and incident notification

risk_description: The organization lacks a centralized inventory of all AI and ML systems in production
current_controls: Partial asset inventory maintained by Engineering
gaps_and_recommendations: Conduct an AI system discovery exercise; build and maintain a living AI inventory with risk tier classification

risk_description: AI systems are not consistently tiered by potential harm level leading to over- or under-resourcing of controls
current_controls: No formal tiering methodology in place
gaps_and_recommendations: Adopt a risk tiering rubric (e.g. based on autonomy data sensitivity and decision impact); apply retroactively to existing systems

risk_description: Models may use features that are statistical proxies for race gender or other protected characteristics without explicit review
current_controls: No systematic proxy variable review in model development lifecycle
gaps_and_recommendations: Require proxy variable analysis as a pre-launch gate; document findings in model cards

risk_description: No structured process for assessing downstream societal or individual harm before AI system launch
current_controls: Privacy Impact Assessments (PIAs) for some systems (not all)
gaps_and_recommendations: Extend PIA process to cover AI-specific harm dimensions; require AI Impact Assessment for all Tier 1 and Tier 2 systems

risk_description: AI systems are not systematically tested for adversarial inputs prompt injection or model evasion prior to deployment
current_controls: Functional QA testing only; no red-teaming
gaps_and_recommendations: Integrate adversarial ML testing and red-teaming into pre-launch security review; document results

risk_description: AI systems lack documented baseline performance metrics making degradation detection impossible
current_controls: Informal monitoring by owning team
gaps_and_recommendations: Define and document baseline accuracy fairness and reliability metrics per system at launch; store in model registry

risk_title: Fairness Metrics Not Tracked in Production
risk_description: Model performance is not disaggregated by demographic subgroup in production monitoring
current_controls: Aggregate performance metrics tracked
gaps_and_recommendations: Implement disaggregated monitoring dashboards; define fairness thresholds and alert on breach

risk_description: No automated process exists to detect statistical drift in model inputs or outputs over time
current_controls: Manual periodic reviews by owning team
gaps_and_recommendations: Deploy automated drift detection with alerting; define drift thresholds per model tier

risk_description: AI models are reviewed only by the teams that built them with no independent technical or ethical review
current_controls: Peer code review within team
gaps_and_recommendations: Establish independent model review process for Tier 1 systems; engage external auditors annually

risk_title: No User Feedback Loop for AI Decisions
risk_description: Users affected by AI-driven decisions have no structured mechanism to flag errors or request review
current_controls: General customer support channel
gaps_and_recommendations: Build AI-specific feedback and dispute mechanism; route to model owner for root cause analysis

risk_title: AI Incidents Not Classified or Tracked
risk_description: No incident classification framework distinguishes AI-related failures from general system incidents
current_controls: General incident response process
gaps_and_recommendations: Define AI incident taxonomy; integrate AI incident tracking into existing IRM tooling

risk_description: Existing incident response playbooks do not address AI-specific failure modes such as model poisoning or hallucination cascades
current_controls: General IRP applies by default
gaps_and_recommendations: Develop AI incident response playbook covering model failure modes bias incidents and data poisoning; table-top test annually

risk_title: No Rollback Capability for AI Models
risk_description: Once a model is deployed there is no documented process to roll back to a prior version in response to an incident
current_controls: Manual re-deployment possible but undocumented
gaps_and_recommendations: Implement model versioning with documented rollback procedures; test rollback capability quarterly

risk_description: No formal process governs the decommissioning of AI systems including data retention model deletion and downstream impact
current_controls: Ad hoc system retirement by owning team
gaps_and_recommendations: Define an AI system decommissioning checklist; integrate with data retention policy

risk_description: Accepted residual risks for AI systems are not formally documented or signed off by an authorized risk owner
current_controls: No formal residual risk sign-off process
gaps_and_recommendations: Require documented residual risk acceptance for all Tier 1 systems; retain in risk register
